issue_title: Bdist_pex failure
issue_body:
Seeing the following error:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "setup.py", line 29, in <module>
        zip_safe=False,
      File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/distutils/core.py", line 148, in setup
        dist.run_commands()
      File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/distutils/dist.py", line 955, in run_commands
        self.run_command(cmd)
      File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.1/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/distutils/dist.py", line 974, in run_command
        cmd_obj.run()
      File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pex/commands/bdist_pex.py", line 68, in run
        for script in self.distribution.entry_points.get('console_scripts', []))
    AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'get'

This is happening because of the following in setup.py:

    setup(
        <...>,
        entry_points='''
            [console_scripts]
            foo = foo.cli:cli
       ''',
    )

This usage is valid. From https://pythonhosted.org/setuptools/setuptools.html#dynamic-discovery-of-services-and-plugins: "The entry_points argument to setup() accepts either a string with .ini-style sections, or a dictionary mapping entry point group names to either strings or lists of strings containing entry point specifiers."

There may be a more robust way to get the list of entry points from setuptools. Otherwise, I can PR a graceful `die()`?

issue_title: pex cannot resolve dependencies when provided with files (bdists) as inputs

In the Bazel world, external dependencies (e.g. an .egg or .whl) are explicitly declared and fetched for you via an http_file() or other similar target analog. As a result of this, the inputs available to pex at the point of invocation consist of bdists (egg/whl) with no capabilities for transitive resolution or fetching.

issue_title: Reduce compilation invalidation scope of targets with strict_deps=True
issue_body: Now, `strict_deps` will reduce the compile classpath used, but it isn't taken into account when generating fingerprints for compile. Doing so will further improve the performance benefit of strict_deps.

issue_title: Lazily extract zjars before incremental compile
Node bundles use filesystem dependencies to pull in transitive node modules
issue_body:
## Problem

Node modules built with pants that contain transitive dependencies are not widely reusable on machines other than the one that built them.

## Explanation

Let's say I have two node targets in my build.  For example, [this node_bundle](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/node/examples/src/node/web-component-button/BUILD#L49-L52) targets [this node module](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/node/examples/src/node/web-component-button/BUILD#L3-L11) which, transitively, depends on [this other node module](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/node/examples/src/node/web-build-tool/BUILD).

When I bundle this together, I get back a tar ball.  If I go into the tar ball and examine the package.json file, it contains an entry like this:

```
{
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "0.13.3", 
    "web-build-tool": "*/path/to/my/pants/clone/*.pants.d/resolve/node/252d64521cf9/contrib.node.examples.src.node.web-build-tool.web-build-tool/current", 
    "mocha": "2.3.0"
  }
```

So, if I publish that tar ball and try to use it on a machine other than the one that built it, npm will fail on parsing the dependencies, since it sees that one of them _should_ exist in the filesystem, but does not.

Is there an existing way to make the published tarball usable on other machines?  If not, I'm willing to take a swing at fixing this, any help/input would be greatly appreciated.

issue_title: use v2 process execution for c/c++ compile and link tasks
issue_body:
The `CCompile`, `CppCompile`, and `LinkSharedLibraries` tasks are introduced in #5815 to compile and link C and C++ code. These classes use the `subprocess` module to execute the compiler or linker, because that PR was made before we had output files for processes. The processes should wrap their `BinaryTool`s in `Snapshot`s, and obtain a `Snapshot` for those tools and the source files, then collect the output files.

Output object file names are predictable (`<file extension>` -> `.o`) for the compilation tasks, and the output file is given as a `-o` argument to the link task.

issue_title: Add a section in pants docsite about "strict_deps" and "exports"
issue_body:
strict_deps and exports can be confusing to users. We should have a dedicated section on our docsite to explain them.

The questions we want to answer there is:
1. What is strict_deps?
2. Why strict_deps?
3. What is exports literal?
4. Why exports?
5. When to use strict_deps and exports?

issue_title: GoBuildgen is too passive when generating FLOATING deps
issue_body:
Right now the fact a dep is floating is logged in yellow like so:
```
3rdparty/golang/github.com/Medium/napping/BUILD (github.com/Medium/napping) 509a46a071456ac766536bcfe99557b052fc624f
3rdparty/golang/github.com/robertkrimen/otto/BUILD (github.com/robertkrimen/otto) FLOATING
```

The 1st line is green, good pinned, the 2nd yellow, with the implication of warning not good.  This is subtle though as pointed out by @kellyellis.

Make the dange of floaters more clear.  Ideas include:
1. s/yellow/red/
2. Add a post-script to the output that has a note about the danger and what to do to fix it.
3. Add a flag that allows a repo to make the choice to have floaters fail the build.

issue_title: Support deprecation for more than just options
issue_body:
The discussion in https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2650/ put us on a path where it would be nice if we could deprecate things external to pants (pants.ini config, command-line options, things in BUILD files) in such a way that users upgrading from any arbitrary version of pants, to any future version of pants, could be notified in the deprecations in a nice way.

This was further discussed in slack:

https://pantsbuild.slack.com/archives/general/p1440451305004760

The idea seems to be to create a tool (either inside of pants or outside), that pants developers can register deprecated things (which will be stored for all time), and that pants admins can use when upgrading pants in their respective repo.

issue_title: Eagerly fetch stdout, stderr from remote execution responses
issue_body:
Right now when remote execution finishes, we just return whether it was successful, and any inline stdout/stderr.

Instead, we should fetch the stdout and stderr if it's not inline, and populate those fields.

This involves editing [this code](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/628c83d5ab2706b0f64d69568c57a718ec7c5e2a/src/rust/engine/process_execution/src/remote.rs#L219-L223) to check whether `stdout_raw` or `stdout` is set, and if the latter, fetch it and populate the value. Same for stderr.

issue_title: Possible to attach customer-managed policies?
issue_body:
Hi @sanathkr,

The spec says that `Policies` can be a `string | List of string` equal to `Names of AWS managed IAM policies`. Does that mean that currently it is not possible to do attach customer-managed policies created in a different stack?

One reason to do that is to move creation of policies to a different stack that requires elevated permissions, and only attach these in the SAM template. Otherwise my SAM template, kept under version control, would need to run with elevated permissions that essentially can create any policy under my account. I feel less comfortable with that, especially if I'm going to solicit public contributions to my project on Github and not every change can be vetted.

Another solution would be to pass Roles, with even fewer privileges but also less flexibility. Basically, I would need to create one Role per function, unlike with policies that can be attached to multiple functions for mix-and-match (I can however do that in the other stack).

Are there any plans to enable specifying customer-managed policies in SAM templates?

Thanks

issue_title: Various prison addresses cause usaddress.tag to bail
issue_body:
I work with databases of addresses of prisons, and there are a number of them that trip up `usaddress.tag`.  Below are the problematic addresses (taken from http://www.dc.state.fl.us/orginfo/facilitydir.html, http://www.tdcj.state.tx.us/unit_directory/, and http://www.bop.gov/locations/list.jsp ).  If there's a more helpful way for me to report/package these failures let me know how.

A few trends:
- "Unit" is taken as `OcupancyType` even when it should sometimes be recipient.
- Weird Texas road names ("FM", "Spur", "Hwy")
- Including both PO Box and street address trips things up.
- Doubly-named streets (such as  700 NE 55th Blvd, State Road 26 East -- "State Road 26 East" is a redundant name of "NE 55th Blvd", but the address as given includes both).

It's an interesting question as to whether the last two (PO Box with street and doubly-named streets) are considered "valid" addresses.  The redundant street info is clearly not needed; but the address as rendered by official sources includes it.  Letters addressed that way would arrive.  So I'd suggest that even though it's weird and _maybe_ non-conformant, `usaddress` as a tool for handling in-the-wild addresses shouldn't bail out when it encounters them.

Some organizations prefer that their users always be logged in

A facility setting can be added, e.g. "Disable guest access".

k0.10.0

issue_title: Some help needed
issue_body:
Hi again,

While I was developing it, my little "secretupdater" app that receives webhooks and pulls secrets from Confidant and pushes Secrets into kubernetes was working well.
Now that I have SAML authentication, rather than the `header` "authentication", I'm having no end of confusion and trouble.

How I've built it:
I'm using the python confidant client as a library, filling in the config this way, with the values pulled from env variables:
```
client = ConfidantClient(
            url=app.config.get('CONFIDANT_SERVER_URL'),
            auth_key=app.config.get('CONFIDANT_SERVER_AUTH_KEY'),
            auth_context=app.config.get('CONFIDANT_SERVER_AUTH_CONTEXT'),
            assume_role=app.config.get('CONFIDANT_SERVER_IAM_ROLE'),
            region=app.config.get('CONFIDANT_SERVER_AWS_REGION'),
            token_cache_file=''  # Not caching the token
        )
```
On receiving the webhook from Confidant, the secretupdater extracts the affected services, then goes through and pulls all the credentials for each affected service, with:
```
for service in affected_services:
    service_details = client.get_service(service=service)
```
(At least, it would, if it worked.)

Then it converts them to k8s Secret format and pushes those into k8s.

I'm now managed to confuse myself (and my colleague) about what exactly is happening when the confidant client talks to confidant.
It seems like I've got some problem involving IAM, KMS and roles interacting with grants, but I don't really follow what's happening.
Assuming we have a Confidant server running on an instance that has an IAM instance role, and we want a client, running on the same instance to be able to access Confidant credentials as mentioned above, would you mind describing the flow of what is happening, what options I need to use to access all services' credentials from this client, and what the involvement with KMS Grants and IAM Roles are?

I'd really appreciate any light you can shed on this - I've been trying all sorts of permutations on the config options, and I keep getting errors like:
```
ERROR:root:Failed to validate token.
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/srv/confidant/confidant/keymanager.py", line 146, in decrypt_token
    EncryptionContext=context
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/botocore/client.py", line 159, in _api_call

issue_body:
Originally reported by @mitechie via IRC

It seems that connecting to self-hosted controllers with macaroon auth fails with the following error:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "examples/connect_current_model.py", line 26, in <module>
    loop.run(main())
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/loop.py", line 38, in run
    raise task.exception()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/asyncio/tasks.py", line 239, in _step
    result = coro.send(None)
  File "examples/connect_current_model.py", line 16, in main
    await model.connect()
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/model.py", line 507, in connect
    await self._connector.connect_model(model_name, **kwargs)
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connector.py", line 140, in connect_model
    bakery_client=self.bakery_client_for_controller(controller_name),
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connector.py", line 68, in connect
    self._connection = await Connection.connect(**kwargs)
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connection.py", line 168, in connect
    await self._connect_with_redirect([(endpoint, cacert)])
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connection.py", line 511, in _connect_with_redirect
    login_result = await self._connect_with_login(endpoints)
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connection.py", line 483, in _connect_with_login
    result = (await self.login())['response']
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connection.py", line 538, in login
    "params": params,
  File "/home/johnsca/juju/python-libjuju/juju/client/connection.py", line 316, in rpc
    raise errors.JujuAPIError(result)
juju.errors.JujuAPIError: no credentials provided
```

We confirmed that the macaroon is indeed being sent, and after some additional testing I discovered that the `auth-tag` is required, even though that info is encoded into the macaroon.  As it doesn't hurt anything to send it anyway, we should just unconditionally supply it.

Dual Vendor Network (Arista / Juniper).
ISIS-SegmentRouting for MPLS enabled.

ExaBGP receives the BGP-LS exported ISIS-SR routes. The Arista EOS (4.21.0F) devices export multiple IP addresses (Loopback + all local IPs) that subsequently are filled into the "local-te-router-id" field of the json message. On Juniper only one of these fields exists. Which is the actual router-id.
However, the local-te-router-id field exists multiple times as visible in the following output. When using a json library to parse this string, abviousely only the last processed value remains (keys will be overwritten).
I'm not sure if the behaviour of the Arista devices is correct or not. If so, ExaBGP should return these values as an array. If this behaviour is not RFC complient, ExaBGP should probably do something different then returning multiple times the same key within the dictionary.

issue_title: Ubuntu 14.10: system-wide pip is broken by docker-py
issue_body:
My case may seem bad practice for some part of the community, but I really need to provide an access to the Docker Python API for a running docker container (e.g. mount /var/run/docker.sock into container). Our team use Ubuntu 14.10 both for development and production. So I just wanted to install the *latest* version of **docker-py** directly to system directories (using system-wide **pip**). I didn't want to resort to virtualenv inside a container because it's an excess level of isolation.

Unfortunately installation of **docker-py** anywhere outside the virtualenv leads to **pip** corruption on the recent version of Ubuntu. I guess that the reason is broken backward compatibility of **requests** module.
```bash
$ docker run -it --rm ubuntu:utopic bash
$ apt-get update
$ apt-get install -y python-pip
$ pip install docker-py
$ pip freeze
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/pip", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('pip==1.5.6', 'console_scripts', 'pip')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources.py", line 356, in load_entry_point
    return get_distribution(dist).load_entry_point(group, name)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources.py", line 2476, in load_entry_point
    return ep.load()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources.py", line 2190, in load
    ['__name__'])
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pip/__init__.py", line 61, in <module>
    from pip.vcs import git, mercurial, subversion, bazaar  # noqa
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pip/vcs/mercurial.py", line 9, in <module>
    from pip.download import path_to_url
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pip/download.py", line 25, in <module>
    from requests.compat import IncompleteRead
ImportError: cannot import name IncompleteRead
```
There are a lot of workarounds here (install virtualenv inside container, etc), but probably it's possible just to reconfigure docker-py for the mentioned distribution.

issue_title: Pipeline instances create incomplete GPOS
issue_body:
copied from a report I received:
"This was found while checking the validity of the GSUB and GPOS in fonts created by pipeline. The designer states: ŌĆśMyanmar is used in two other languages, Mon and Sgaw Karen, which are accounted for in our GPOS and GSUB. Pipeline seems to drop the two scripts from the GPOS.
GPOS in Pipeline Instances DO NOT carry over to Script Locales.

The code below was dumped from the PipelineŌĆÖs Noto Sans Myanmar RegularŌĆÖs GPOS. The Regular instance is supposed to be equivalent in weight and width to the Noto Sans Myanmar Regular Master made by Monotype.
 
script table begin
mym2    default         0, 1, 2
script table end
 
However, the script table above is missing two locales present in our Regular Master:
 
script table begin
mym2    default         0, 1, 2
mym2   KSW           0, 1, 2
mym2   MON           0, 1, 2
script table end
 
As a result, when in a different locale, there is NO GPOS positioning taking place in the pipeline generated fonts."


For reference:

- the source in question is in https://github.com/googlei18n/noto-source/tree/master/src/NotoSansMyanmar
- a sample font generated by the pipeline on 2017.March.1 is in https://github.com/googlei18n/noto-fonts/blob/master/alpha/from-pipeline/unhinted/otf/sans/NotoSans-Regular.otf

issue_title: Check running platform version
issue_body:
Platform version is stored in a number of ways throughout Deis.  Checking the version at different stages in a cluster's life cycle varies. A source of truth should be defined to know if an upgrade is needed or if a upgrade was successful.

## Current state of versioning

`/etc/deis-release` is set on initial install but not updated to reflect running version if a platform upgrade is performed.
https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/contrib/coreos/user-data.example#L102-L104

Platform version is set for performing an upgrade.  This is a `desired` version and does not reflect what is running.
http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/managing_deis/upgrading-deis/#in-place-upgrade


## Proposal

Add component versions to `deisctl version` to check container `DEIS_RELEASE` environment variables.

```
Client:
    deisctl:        v1.10.1
API:
    controller:     v1.7
Platform:
	builder:        v1.10.1
	controller:     v1.10.1
	database:       v1.10.1
	logger:         v1.10.1
	logspout:       v1.10.1
	publisher:      v1.10.1
	registry:       v1.10.1
	router:         v1.10.1
	store-daemon:   v1.10.1
	store-gateway:  v1.10.1
	store-metadata: v1.10.1
	store-monitor:  v1.10.1
```

issue_body:
The driving use case is this:
A user with a large number of shares wants to change the local user associated with those shares.
In order to do so, the user needs to create new shares under the new local user and then migrate a large number of ACLs from the old shares to the new ones.

This changes the share IDs, but their attributes can be replicated with a little bit of scripting, so search-based lookups will continue to work for users of the shares.

The key to making this use case work smoothly is allowing `globus transfer endpoint acl add-rule` (or a new, similar command) capable of consuming the JSON output of `globus transfer endpoint acl list`.
I think we can change the behavior of `add-rule` based on a flag, so we get this:
```shell
$ oldshare="aabb"
$ newshare="xxyy"  # not 48, XXYY Syndrome, just a fake ID
$ globus transfer endpoint acl list --endpoint-id "$oldshare" --format JSON | \
  globus transfer endpoint acl add-rule --from-list --endpoint-id "$newshare"
```

This is highly desirable for a very small number of users.
Potentially offering a solution purely through the SDK is more desirable, but this seems like a clear-cut case for "added value" on top of core SDK features. We shouldn't pollute it with rarefied usage like this.

issue_title: Add `--notify` option to Transfer Task submission
issue_body:
The Transfer API supports notification settings whenever a Task is submitted:
`notify_on_succeeded`, `notify_on_failed`, and `notify_on_inactive`

Exposing this in the CLI could go down a number of routes, but here's one option:
`--notify off`: no notifications, all set to False
`--notify on`: (default) all set to True
`--notify "succeeded,failed"`: don't notify when tasks become inactive
`--notify failed`: notify on when tasks fail
`--notify "off,succeeded"`: UsageError
`--notify "succeeded,failed,inactive"`: same as default, but explicitly set these to True (vs. omitting from API doc)

I don't want to get too fancy about having options which are, themselves, parsed (vs. `--notify` being an option where we allow multiple usage), but `--notify off --notify failed` seems inherently ambiguous. Not allowing multiple uses and following `click`'s typical "last use wins" logic 

Relates to globusonline/koa#62

issue_title: Both `VM` and `State` classes define a `block_class`
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Both the `VM` and `State` (aka `VMState`) objects implement a `block_class` or (`_block_class` from the `VM`) property and API.  There should only be one `block_class` defined for a given VM ruleset.

### How can it be fixed

- Remove the `block_class` API from the `VM` class and change all usage to delegate to the `State` class API.
- Change the `State` class to have a `get_block_class()` which is a `classmethod` for retrieval of the class instead of directly accessing it via the attribute.

issue_body:

We can merge `sharding` branch into `master` branch to reduce additional overheads.

1. Rebase sharding branch
2. Clean up
3. Make a PR to make sure the sharding stuff is isolated enough

issue_title: Update binary trie with new data structure
issue_body:
As proposed and discussed in [ethresearch](https://ethresear.ch/t/data-availability-proof-friendly-state-tree-transitions/1453/6), Binary Trie built on Sparse Merkle Tree has the advantage that it's more simple and straight forward(there only exist one node type - branch node, just like plain Binary Tree) while size of merkle proof is roughly the same(or even slightly better) since most subtree in Sparse Merkle Tree would be empty and we can compress these nodes into 1 bit representation.

The cost is that it takes O(N) time to `get`/`set` value. In our case(with 2^160 address space) it's 160 nodes to traverse to get to the leaf node, but there should be room for optimization.

issue_title: Creating environment with some pip arguments (not supported in requirements.txt) broken in 4.4+
issue_body:
Broken in https://github.com/conda/conda/pull/3969 , specifically https://github.com/conda/conda/commit/ebd0f1aae686117790ef8e688829ee2776825c6d First breakage is that the lines used to just be "appended" and supplied to `pip`, so something like ```yaml name: "myenv" dependencies: - "python=3.6" - "pip=10.0.1" - "requests=2.18.4" - pip: - "--index-url" - "https://corp.repo/pypi-internal/" - "pyyaml==3.11" ``` worked. Now, it would produce something like `pip: error: --index-url option requires an argument` due to the changes mentioned above. To work now, it is now required to be `"--index-url https://packages.physiq.zone/nexus/repository/pypi-internal/simple"` Second breakage is that arguments supported by `pip` CLI are not supported by the [requirements.txt file format](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/reference/pip_install/#requirements-file-format), so arguments like: ```yaml name: "myenv" dependencies: - "python=3.6" - "pip=10.0.1" - "requests=2.18.4" - pip: - "pyyaml==3.11" - "--retries" - 3 - "--timeout" - 60 ``` worked. With the changes mentioned above, other options like `--proxy`, `--trusted-host`, etc. (from [here](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/reference/pip/?highlight=retries#general-options)) cannot be used. **I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior Installing of `pip` fails due to unsupported options ### Steps to Reproduce ``` cat << EOF > /tmp/myenv.yaml name: "myenv" dependencies: - "python=3.6" - "pip=10.0.1" - "requests=2.18.4" - pip: - "--retries 3" - "--timeout 60" EOF conda create --prefix /tmp/myenv --yes conda env update --prune --prefix /tmp/myenv --file /tmp/myenv.yaml Solving environment: done ==> WARNING: A newer version of conda exists. <== current version: 4.5.1 latest version: 4.5.3 Please update conda by running $ conda update -n base conda Downloading and Extracting Packages pip 10.0.1################################################################################################################################################################################################# | 100% Preparing transaction: done Verifying transaction: done Executing transaction: done Usage: pip [options] Invalid requirement: --retries 3 pip: error: no such option: --retries ``` ## Expected Behavior Using `pip` arguments that are supported by `pip` but not in a `requirements.txt` should continue to be successful. ## Environment Information

issue_title: Non-deterministic behavior when solving simple environment
issue_body:
## Current Behavior I'm currently seeing non-deterministic behavior when building conda environments from multiple channels. I've trimmed it down to an example with just two dependencies: conda-forge::openmpi and cryoem::fftw-mpi. The cryoem channel also has their own openmpi recipe, and when creating the environment, this recipe is sometimes chosen over the one from conda-forge, despite the set channel precedence and channel information specified in the dependency list. I've found a number of issues related to non-deterministic behavior on the conda and conda-build issue lists, but wanted to add this one because it is particularly simple and occurring with recent versions of conda. Following some of those issues, I have found that fixing PYTHONHASHSEED to 1 or 2 results in the build deterministically pulling openmpi from cryoem and conda-forge, respectively. ### Steps to Reproduce ``` condo env create -f env-mpi-error.yaml ``` where the contents of the environment file are: ``` name: mpi-error channels: - conda-forge - cryoem - defaults dependencies: - conda-forge::openmpi - cryoem::fftw-mpi ``` ## Expected Behavior I'm hoping to get openmpi from conda-forge, which I think should be enforced by specifying the channel explicitly in the dependency list. ## Environment Information

issue_title: No conda commands work
issue_body:
Hi all,

Upon trying basically any conda command, I get the following error:

```
Fetching package metadata ...
CondaHTTPError: HTTP None None for url <https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/repodata.json.bz2>
Elapsed: None

An HTTP error occurred when trying to retrieve this URL.
HTTP errors are often intermittent, and a simple retry will get you on your way.
SSLError(SSLError(SSLError("bad handshake: SysCallError(10054, 'WSAECONNRESET')",),),)
```
After some searching around here for similar issues I have tried turning off SSL Verify using this command:
`conda config --set ssl_verify false`

...which succeeds but does not help the problem.  I **can** get to the https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/win-64/ path via a browser and download files directly, but cannot install or update using conda.  Can't do conda update conda, etc.

I assume my company's firewall setup is preventing this -- but I'm not sure what to test to get to the real root cause.  Any thoughts?

issue_title: TabixFile fails silently on data indexed by old tabix versions
Error when mate unmapped
issue_body:
While dealing with RNA-seq bam file from TCGA i got this error iterating over the bam file using HTSeq:

File "python2/src/HTSeq/_HTSeq.pyx", line 1338, in HTSeq._HTSeq.SAM_Alignment.from_pysam_AlignedSegment (src/_HTSeq.c:30737)
  File "pysam/libcalignmentfile.pyx", line 1635, in pysam.libcalignmentfile.AlignmentFile.getrname (pysam/libcalignmentfile.c:18952)
  File "pysam/libcalignmentfile.pyx", line 699, in pysam.libcalignmentfile.AlignmentFile.get_reference_name (pysam/libcalignmentfile.c:9684)
ValueError: reference_id -1 out of range 0<=tid<2779

The problem seems due to this kind of reads in the bam file:

UNC11-SN627_70:1:1101:3483:1995	69	*	0	0	*	*	0	0	NGGTGCTTTATTCTCCACAGAGTGATACATGCTAAGGTGGGTTGGGCTTG	#:99:;<<<;D:7DDDDDDDDD@DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD6:DDDDDDDD	NH:i:0	HI:i:0	AS:i:49	nM:i:0	uT:A:4	RG:Z::110325_UNC11-SN627_0070_AB02DVABXX.1

The 69 flag tells the read is unmapped while the mate is mapped, however the sam line reports '*' for the mate too which is not a valid chromosome and is not in the ref sequences that indeed are 2779

Is it possible to fix it to handle these kind of excepions?
Thanx

issue_title: Validate body
issue_body:
In my use cases, the `body` param has always needed to be a `dict`. On a couple occasions I accidentally passed something else (say a list) and got some sort of `TransportError, Failed to derive xcontent`. I propose adding a simple validation (perhaps in `Transport.perform_request`) to make these errors more easily identified.

Off the top of my head I'm not sure if `body` is always a dict. If it is this should be straightforward. Otherwise it would take a bit more work, but I think we should have that discussion.

issue_title: Ability to get/set continuation token
issue_body:
Greetings. I am currently developing an API that queries documents in a collection, and returns the results. I would like to add functionality for obtaining the next page. From what I understand about DocumentDB, a continuation token is returned, in addition to the queried documents, if there are more results.

My thoughts are: obtain the token after the results are retrieved and return the token to the client. The client may then use the token in the next request to obtain the next page.

After doing some digging, I found a section of the source code that sets the execution context's continuation token. Working backwards, I ended up writing code like this:

```
query = {'query': 'select * from c '}
options = {'continuation': continuation} if continuation else {}

query_iterable = client.QueryDocuments(collection_link, query, options)
results = query_iterable.fetch_next_block()
continuation = query_iterable._options.get('continuation')
```

Unfortunately this does not seem to work for several reasons:

1.) First off, the value for `continuation` is always `None`, unless I make another `fetch_next_block()` call after the first one. (I have several thousand documents so there is an expected next page of results)
2.) Even though I set continuation in the options, I still get the same results as before, and the value obtained for the continuation token is the same as the value that I originally sent.

My questions:

1.) What am I doing wrong in my code? It seems fairly straightforward, and yet it doesn't accomplish what I am trying to do.
2.) Clearly this library handles this behavior internally, so are there any plans for exposing this functionality for general use?

Any suggestions/help is greatly appreciated. Thanks!

issue_title: Lots of memory errors when attempting to upload/download large files
issue_body: When attempting to upload or download a large file (such as a 10GB file) I am constantly running into MemoryErrors. Looking at how much memory Python is using during this time I see that it is right around 2GB. This seems like a lot of memory taken to process a 10GB file. Additionally, 2GB of memory use shouldn't be enough to cause these failures in the python runtime (although I imagine that is just a setting somewhere). Ideally we shouldn't be scaling memory usage to be 20% of the file size, since we need to support the ability to upload files in the terabyte range (for upload and download).

issue_title: Baum-Welch for multinomial HMM converges to local optima
issue_body:
In generating sequences from a multinomial (really, Bernoulli) HMM and then learning the parameters back with hmmlearn, the parameters are learned roughly correctly some of the time (about half) and very incorrectly the rest of the time. This holds true regardless of the number of sample sequences (numSeqs in the code below). For reference, Matlab's HMM fitting function correctly fits the parameters for the same model every time it is run. My best guess is that the Baum-Welch algorithm underlying the parameter fitting in hmmlearn is converging to local optima. It would be very helpful if there were safeguards to break out of local optima so that parameter fitting could be counted upon to work every time.


`import numpy as np

from hmmlearn.hmm import MultinomialHMM

pT = .1
pL0 = .3
pG = .25
pS = .05

model = MultinomialHMM(n_components=2)
model.startprob_  = np.array([1.0-pL0, pL0])
model.transmat_ = np.array([[1.0-pT,pT],[0,1]])
model.n_features = 2
model.emissionprob_ = np.array([[1.0-pG, pG], [pS, 1.0 - pS]])


numSeqs = 100

for n in range(10):
  print '\niteration ', n

  seqs = []
  seqLengths = []

  for i in range(numSeqs):
#seqLength = np.random.randint(10,50)
    seqLength = 20
    seqLengths.append(seqLength)
    seq = model.sample(seqLength)[0]
    if len(seqs) > 0:
      seqs = np.append(seqs, seq, axis=0)
    else: seqs = seq

  seqs = np.array(seqs)
  seqLengths = np.array(seqLengths)

  model2 = MultinomialHMM(n_components=2)
  model2.fit(seqs, seqLengths)
  print 'transition: '
  print model2.transmat_
  print 'emission: '
  print model2.emissionprob_

`

issue_title: XSS Vulnerability
issue_body:
## Long story short

I've tested a Web Service using aiohttp 2.3.1 as server with Nessus Security Suite.

Test ID 22254 fails. Description of the test (https://www.tenable.com/plugins/index.php?view=single&id=22254):

The remote web server fails to sanitize the contents of an 'Expect'
request header before using it to generate dynamic web content. An
unauthenticated, remote attacker may be able to leverage this issue to
launch cross-site scripting attacks against the affected service,
perhaps through specially crafted ShockWave (SWF) files.

## Expected behaviour

```
HTTP/1.1 417 Expectation Failed
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
Content-Length: 15
Date: Mon, 06 Nov 2017 08:14:30 GMT
Server: Python/3.5 aiohttp/2.3.1
Connection: close

Unknown Expect.
```

## Actual behaviour

```
HTTP/1.1 417 Expectation Failed
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8
Content-Length: 64
Date: Mon, 06 Nov 2017 08:29:15 GMT
Server: Python/3.5 aiohttp/2.3.1
Connection: close

Unknown Expect: www_expect_xss.nasl testing for BID 19661 <test>
```

## Steps to reproduce
```
nc 127.0.0.1 80 <<EOF
GET / HTTP/1.1
Host: 127.0.0.1
Accept-Charset: iso-8859-1,utf-8;q=0.9,*;q=0.1
Accept-Language: en
Connection: Close
User-Agent: Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 8.0; Windows NT 5.1; Trident/4.0)
Pragma: no-cache
Expect: www_expect_xss.nasl testing for BID 19661 <test>
Accept: image/gif, image/x-xbitmap, image/jpeg, image/pjpeg, image/png, */*


EOF
```

## Your environment

Linux 4.4.0
Pyhton 3.5.2
Aiohttp 2.3.1 as server

## Patch

```
--- a/web_urldispatcher.py	2016-09-29 08:41:39.753246307 +0200
+++ b/web_urldispatcher.py	2017-11-06 09:49:48.852026863 +0100
@@ -191,7 +191,7 @@
         if expect.lower() == "100-continue":
             request.transport.write(b"HTTP/1.1 100 Continue\r\n\r\n")
         else:
-            raise HTTPExpectationFailed(text="Unknown Expect: %s" % expect)
+            raise HTTPExpectationFailed(text="Unknown Expect.")
 
 
 class ResourceAdapter(AbstractResource):
```

## My opinion

Aiohttp sets the "content-type" correctly to "text/plain", so in my opinion a crafted request should not be as bad as descripted in Nessus. But in any case it would be more secure to not return the except request. I will let you decide if it's an aiohttp bug or a Nessus bug (Nessus is propably not checking the content-type).

The problem can be also avoided by implementing a custom "ExpectHandler" for each route.

issue_title: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'cancel'
issue_body:
## Long story short

With recent 1.3 release we start to receive this kind of crash report. 

```
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'cancel'
  File "gns3server/controller/compute.py", line 502, in _run_http_query
    response = yield from self._session().request(method, url, headers=headers, data=data, auth=self._auth, chunked=chunked, timeout=timeout)
  File "aiohttp/client.py", line 583, in __iter__
    resp = yield from self._coro
  File "aiohttp/client.py", line 279, in _request
    resp.connection.add_callback(handle.cancel)

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'cancel'
```
The problem start with this commit: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/commit/4be504377daa3c535c488ad30bd50be48ffedeb7

The problem seem to arrive when we pass timeout=None to request. But it doesn't seem to be always the case.

The problem in _request is if you pass None to the TimeoutHandle you will get a None handle
```python
# timeout is cumulative for all request operations
# (request, redirects, responses, data consuming)
tm = TimeoutHandle(timeout)
handle = tm.handle(self._loop)
```

And we will use this handle later:
```python
        if resp.connection is not None:
            resp.connection.add_callback(handle.cancel)
        else:
            handle.cancel()
```

The code of handle in TimeoutHandle:
```python
    def handle(self, loop):
        if self._timeout is not None and self._timeout > 0:
            at = ceil(loop.time() + self._timeout)
            return loop.call_at(at, self.__call__)
```


Is it wrong to pass None as a timeout?

issue_title: after one TimeoutError all other requests throw TimeoutError
issue_body:
## Long story short
Hi!
Faced with same [problem](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44758283/i-cant-use-aiohttp-session-if-previous-query-failed-with-timeout)
<!-- Please describe your problem and why the fix is important. -->

## Steps to reproduce
```
import aiohttp
import asyncio
import logging


logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG,
                    format='%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s')


async def main():
    asyncio.ensure_future(
        load_page('https://www.netflix.com:5000'))

    asyncio.ensure_future(
        load_page('http://bash.im/', 10))

        load_page('https://myshows.me/'))

        load_page('http://www.lostfilm.tv/'))


async def load_page(url, timeout=3):
    try:
        async with session.get(url, timeout=timeout) as response:
            text = await response.text()
            print(len(text))

    except Exception:
        logging.warning(type(e))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

    conn = aiohttp.TCPConnector(limit=1)
    session = aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=conn, loop=loop)

    asyncio.ensure_future(main())

<!-- Please describe steps to reproduce the issue.

issue_title: Add 'description' to fields and models
issue_body:
Descriptions are a fundamental aspect of a well-designed schema.  They are not only great documentation for other developers, they can be programmatically inspected to populate UIs, etc. They're a key part of the JSON Schema specification and so their addition to `schematics` would enable tools like [jsonschematics](https://github.com/voidfiles/jsonschematics) to create more compliant output, which would in turn make it more compatible with other tools that take JSON Schema documents as an input.

For types, the description makes sense as a new argument to `BaseType.__init__`.  For `Model`s, the value could be taken from the object's `__doc__` attribute (or perhaps just the first sentence).

Thanks for listening.

issue_title: Validation
issue_body:
There are a number of open issues regarding model validation. Unfortunately, it's an aspect of Schematics that's perhaps the most difficult to work on because the current system is somewhat confusing.

In addition to the issues already reported, there are a few details I'd like to ask about:

* Validating a model will reset any empty fields to their default values:

  ```python
  class M(Model):
      n = IntType(default=123)
  ```
  ```python
  >>> m = M()
  >>> m.n = None
  >>> m.to_primitive()
  {'n': None}
  >>> m.validate()
  >>> m.to_primitive()
  {'n': 123}
  ```

  Bug or not?

  (The first version of the Undefined feature introduced a new parameter called `apply_defaults`, which can be used to control this when importing and validating. However, the appropriate default setting for validation depends on whether the current behavior is by design or accident.)

* By the time `_check_for_unknown_fields()` is called from the main `validate()` function, `import_loop` will have already checked for rogue fields.

  What is then the purpose of `_check_for_unknown_fields()`?

* If any type-level validator fails, no model-level validators will be called.

  Bug or not?

issue_title: How to convert defgrad to 1.2?
issue_body:
I upgraded to version 1.2 and switched from `defgrad` to the new `defvjp`. I didn't modify the actual gradient calculation function in any way. However, unit tests that are using `check_grads` to check the gradient numerically started suddenly failing. Also, some other unit tests that depend on the gradient of the function started failing. So, I guess I've done this conversion somehow incorrectly. I couldn't find any information on how to convert `defgrad` to the new `defvjp` (I only found this guide about switching from old `defvjp` to new `defvjp`: https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/docs/updateguide.md#gradient-checking).

So, if I had a function:
```
@primitive
def f(x):
    y = ...
    return y

def df(y, x):
    def g(dy):
        d = ...
        return d
    return g

f.defgrad(df)
```

I just changed `defgrad` method to `defvjp` function, nothing else changed:
```
@primitive
def f(x):
    y = ...
    return y

def df(y, x):
    def g(dy):
        d = ...
        return d
    return g

defvjp(f, df)
```

What am I doing wrong? What else should be modified?

issue_body:
I just updated planemo, and ran into a dependency problem under Python 2.6:

```
$ planemo --version
planemo, version 0.20.1.dev0
$ planemo shed_diff -t toolshed tools/ncbi_blast_plus/
Problem loading command shed_diff, exception cannot import name UserDict
Usage: planemo [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Error: No such command "shed_diff".
```

The ``UserDict`` class moved from Python 2 to 3, which ought to be handled by ``from six.moves import UserDict`` as used in ``planemo_ext/galaxy/util/odict.py``, however my version of six is too old:

```
$ python
Python 2.6.6 (r266:84292, Jul 23 2015, 15:22:56) 
[GCC 4.4.7 20120313 (Red Hat 4.4.7-11)] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from six.moves import UserDict
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: cannot import name UserDict
>>> import six
>>> six.__version__
'1.6.1'
```

Therefore I think that ``requirements.txt`` should set a minimum version of six. The UserDict move was added in version 1.7.0, the current version is 1.10.0.

I manually updated to six v1.10.0 and reinstalled planemo, and this works again.

issue_title: a coherent set of config variables/flags
issue_body:
@olson-sean-k and I had a discussion in https://phabricator.buildinspace.com/D197, and I'm just copying the result of that here, partly in case we lose the Phabricator server :)

We want to make the following configs available. Each one determines all the ones that come after it, unless those are explicitly set too.

- `$PERU_FILE` `--peru-file`: The filepath to the YAML file to use, instead of searching for `peru.yaml`.
- `$PERU_SYNC_DIR` `--sync-dir`: The directory where the root of the imports tree goes.
- `$PERU_STATE_DIR` `--state-dir`: Where to put stuff that normally goes in the `.peru` dir.
- `$PERU_CACHE_DIR` `--cache-dir`: Where to put the cache, which normally goes in `cache/` in the state dir.

Note that we are *not* including a config to change the peru file search name. (We'll be getting rid of the existing `$PERU_FILE_NAME` config.) When peru searches for the file on its own -- which works even if it's somewhere above the current directory -- it will always look for "peru.yaml". It's hard to imagine how changing that in a global config could be useful to anyone, and if you're going to set it locally for some projects, you might as well set the full filepath explicitly.

We're also probably going to require that, if either one of `--peru-file` or `--sync-dir` are set, then both must be set. @olson-sean-k pointed out some very confusing cases that could result from allowing either one to take a default value based on the other. I've forgotten what they were, but they were bad :)

issue_title: Content-Length header in GET method
issue_body:
In matrix_client/api.py line 521:

```python
        if headers["Content-Type"] == "application/json":
            content = json.dumps(content)
```

When using GET APIs such as sync(), content=None is passed to _send, but then content becomes a string 'null', so there will be a 'Content-Length: 4' in the request header. In some HTTP server such as lighttpd, it will return a 400 error when there is a Content-Length header in GET method.

issue_title: Override Handler Registry
issue_body:
In the "new" API functions to get data there appears to be no way to override the default handlers. It is great to get data via the 

```python
dataset = db['5933c2']
data_gen = dataset.data('fccd_image')
data = list(data_gen)
```

But there is no way to override the handler to look at the data differently. `get_events`, has a method to override the handler but I thought these methods were now depreciated.

issue_title: Write Spladder/Alt-Splice Pipeline
issue_body:
- variant calling ~ 1h / single thread / 1G RAM
- AS calling ~ 2.5 h / single thread / 5G RAM
- QC run ~10min / single thread / < 1G RAM

I would suggest to lump variant calling and QC together. 

As input data, I have currently used the hg38 Reference genome downloaded from ensembl and as annotation the Gencode V23 comprehensive. Let me know when you need more information. 

Full list of dependencies:

Variant calling step:
===============
Requirements:
* samtools - version 1.3

Pre-requisits:
* index genome.fa with: samtools faidx <genome.fa>
(this needs to be done only once and the genome.fa and the index can be provided as input files)

Call:
* samtools-1.3 mpileup -f [genome.fa] -l [position_file] -v [alignment.bam] -t DP,SP,INFO/AD,INFO/ADF,INFO/ADR,INFO/DPR,SP > [output.vcf]

Resources:
* time: ~ 1h
* memory: ~ 300MB
* disk space: only for result file ~ 10MB

Source:
http://www.htslib.org/download/

Alternative splicing step:
===================
Requirements:
* python 2.7
* pysam
* h5py
* numpy
* scipy

Pre-requisits:
* needs genome.fa
* needs annotation.gtf

Call:
* python spladder.py --insert_ir=y --insert_es=y --insert_ni=y --remove_se=n --validate_sg=n -b [alignment.bam] -o [outdir] -a [annotation.gtf] -v y -c 3 -M merge_graphs -T y -n [readlength] -P y -p n --sparse_bam y
(we can set the read length to constant 50 for now - this will only influence some filter criteria and should be fine - unless you have a large fraction of 32bp reads in your sets, which is unlikely)

Resources:
* time: ~ 2-3h
* memory: ~ 4 GB
* single CPU
* disk space: ~ 1GB	

Source:
https://github.com/ratschlab/spladder

QC tool:
=======
Requirements:
* python 2.7
* pysam
* pandas
* scipy
* numpy

Pre-requisits:
* needs pre-processed annotation table - will be provided by us

Call:
* checkBias_2.0.py -o [outfile-prefix] -n [alignment.bam] -v -a [annotation.gtf] -m [annotation.preprocessed.m53]

Resources:
* time: ~20min
* memory: < 1GB
* disk space: negligible

Source:
* https://github.com/KjongLehmann/m53

issue_title: Independent StagedActivation for different agents?
issue_body:
Dear all,

I currently try to rewrite a NetLogo model with Mesa in Python. So far it works quite well but I have issues with the runtime.
My Setup:
I have to types of agents (seeds and plants). Now I want first to run through all seeds than to runs through all plants and within I want to do some changes on the grid.
My current solution:
At the moment I do this by StagedActivation, but this means I always have to loop over all agents, which takes a lot of time. 
My question: 
Is there any option I overlooked to have several different steps or a stages which do not loop over all agents but only over an agent group?

Thanks for help,

Simone

issue_title: 64-bit vs 32-bit
issue_body:

issue_title: Convince ourselves that temp_install() isn't really a security vuln, or fix it
issue_body:
There's an extremely dramatic security warning comment in the function [temp_install](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L1252) in the apache plugin.

This function performs a filesystem copy operation.  AFAICT the source file is controlled by `setuptools` and the destination is within the apache configuration directory controlled by the command line and/or a Let's Encrypt configuration file.

Since Let's Encrypt is not designed to perform privileged operations for unprivileged users, command lines or configuration files, I believe this is an utter non-issue.  But I'd like someone to check my reasoning on this.

issue_title: `str` doesn't render wkst properly
issue_body:
`rrule.__str__` is rendering `WKST` as an integer instead of as a weekday. For example:

```python
>>> rule = dateutil.rrule.rrulestr("DTSTART:20000101\nFREQ=WEEKLY;WKST=SU")
>>> str(rule)
'DTSTART:20000101T000000\nFREQ=WEEKLY;WKST=6'
```
This breaks compatibility with RFC5545 and also with `rrulestr`:
```python
>>> dateutil.rrule.rrulestr(str(rule))
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: invalid 'WKST': 6
```

issue_title: Ensure that c. coordinate is within CDS bounds
issue_body:
We don't verify that a c. variant is within CDS bounds. This causes bugs in (at least) c_to_p. An example is #425.

At a minimum, we should add bounds checking to the ExtrinsicValidator class.

However, EV isn't invoked during projection because it's a bit expensive. One option is to start running EV by default, but also provide a way for callers to disable validation (i.e., enable them to assert that the variants have already been validated).

issue_title: include_data is always set to False for Relationship fields.
issue_body:
It's not possible to set include_data to True via the constructor.

```
def __init__(
        self,
        related_url='', related_url_kwargs=None,
        self_url='', self_url_kwargs=None,
        include_resource_linkage=False, schema=None,
        many=False, type_=None, id_field=None, **kwargs
    ):
        self.related_url = related_url
        self.related_url_kwargs = related_url_kwargs or {}
        self.self_url = self_url
        self.self_url_kwargs = self_url_kwargs or {}
        if include_resource_linkage and not type_:
            raise ValueError('include_resource_linkage=True requires the type_ argument.')
        self.many = many
        self.include_resource_linkage = include_resource_linkage
        self.include_data = False
        self.__schema = schema
        self.type_ = type_
        self.id_field = id_field or self.id_field
        super(Relationship, self).__init__(**kwargs)
```

should be

```
def __init__(
        self,
        related_url='', related_url_kwargs=None,
        self_url='', self_url_kwargs=None,
        include_resource_linkage=False, 
        include_data=False, schema=None,
        many=False, type_=None, id_field=None, **kwargs
    ):
        self.related_url = related_url
        self.related_url_kwargs = related_url_kwargs or {}
        self.self_url = self_url
        self.self_url_kwargs = self_url_kwargs or {}
        if include_resource_linkage and not type_:
            raise ValueError('include_resource_linkage=True requires the type_ argument.')
        self.many = many
        self.include_resource_linkage = include_resource_linkage
        self.include_data = include_data
        self.__schema = schema
        self.type_ = type_
        self.id_field = id_field or self.id_field
        super(Relationship, self).__init__(**kwargs)
```

issue_title: Activity classifier data prep computes majority, not mode
issue_body:
The activity classifier downsamples the raw data, so that *k* consecutive rows are assigned the same target classification. To compute the chunk's label from the *k* raw labels, it performs the [Boyer-Moore majority vote algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BoyerŌĆōMoore_majority_vote_algorithm). However, this algorithm only guarantees that if the data has a majority value (occurs at least *k/2* times), then it outputs that value. Otherwise, it outputs an arbitrary element.

In particular, suppose *k* = 9 and the raw labels are `ABABABABC`. Then the Boyer-Moore algorithm outputs `C`, instead of one of the two modes.

Since we convert the labels into integers in 0..(num classes - 1), we should just generate a histogram. Computing the mode in this fashion is *O(n+k)* time and *O(k)* space, which for small *k* is just as good as Boyer-Moore's *O(n)* time and *O(1)* space.

issue_title: Enhance the gen --fetch-license option to generate license URL's
issue_body:
When I run `gen` to generate ABOUT files, I use the --fetch-license option, which works fine when I provide the a value in the license_expression column (or map to it), and it validates the license_expression values that I provide and generates the .LICENSE files, and stores the license_expression and .LICENSE file names in the .ABOUT files that get generated -- so far so good. 

However, for the reports that I generate, I also need the URL to each license in my license library.  Right now I do that with by providing values in the license_url column.  Those are easy enough to construct in my input csv by using an Excel CONCATENATE formula, but that only works well when the license_expression references exactly one license key; otherwise I need to do that task manually.  It would be really helpful if the --fetch-license option could capture and save each license_url when it validates the license using the relevant part of api root url that I provide.   Here is an example (somewhat obscured) of the kind of output I would like to see in the .ABOUT file: 

```
license_expression: apache-2.0 AND oasis-ws-security-spec AND w3c AND ws-policy-specification
license_name: |
    Apache License 2.0
    Oasis WS Security Specification License
    W3C Software Notice and License

license_file: |

issue_title: Hydration of many (5000) resources very slow due to deepcopy in Resource.__init__()
issue_body:
I'm doing some profiling to speed up an API that I'm building with tastypie and am seeing ~ 70% (5 of 7 seconds) of my time being spent right now doing the deepcopy of the base fields in Resource.__init__ when hydrating 5000 resources.  

Given the general nature of the Resource object, I guess there are probably cases where you'd have a mutable object in the base Resource and need to create new ones for each instance but is that also true for ModelResources?

issue_title: Putting a foreign key to null is ignored
issue_body:
There is a bug (documented well here: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/9695798/using-tastypie-to-update-a-foreignkey-field-to-null) that means PUTting a foreign key as null will be ignored and not saved to the database - despite both the django and tastypie field specifying null=True. The proceeding GET shows that the value has not changed. Posting works fine.

To anyone interested, a temporary user-level fix is in the aforementioned stack overflow post.

issue_title: Heartbeat problems when running with caffe
issue_body:
Background: I'm running my experiments using Caffe.
I had this weird error that my experiments would not terminate and heartbeat signals got lost without notice. It seems that this is due to the main thread accessing C++ stuff through boost.python bindings and that the heartbeat thread was simply not responding.

I was able to fix the problem by changing the IntervalTimer class
https://github.com/IDSIA/sacred/blob/d71cf7c6294e8b7d0c2a3992bfdf1eaf4cf00652/sacred/utils.py#L443
to inherit from `multiprocessing.Process` instead of `threading.Thread`.

If there's interest for adding this I can add a PR. This change should not break any existing functionality and allow for running experiments which use the boost.python interface.

EDIT: Just noticed that passing objects between processes is not as easy as I thought. I'll have to work out some more magic to make this work

issue_title: Invalid model for string index when symbolic string is not contained in the other string
issue_body:
Hi again, I have another invalid model with strings. Here I am checking that whenever one string does not contain the other, there is no index at which the other string appears as a substring of the first string:

```
(set-option :model-validate true)

(declare-fun c0 () String)
(declare-fun c1 () String)
(declare-fun c2 () Int)
(assert (<= 0 (str.indexof c0 c1 c2)))
(assert (not (str.contains c0 c1)))
(assert (< c2 (str.len c0)))
(check-sat)

(get-model)
```

Z3 returns a negative index, then recognizes that it is an invalid model:

```
sat
(error "line 9 column 10: an invalid model was generated")
(model 
  (define-fun c2 () Int
    (- 1))
  (define-fun c0 () String
    "")
  (define-fun c1 () String
    "\x00")
)
```

I know that str.at is undefined with negative offset -- here it looks like Z3 is generating a negative offset for string.index. Is this defined? The line that fails with that model is:

```
(assert (<= 0 (str.indexof c0 c1 c2)))
```

Should I be constraining c2 to always be greater than or equal to zero myself? 

Thanks again!

Talia

issue_title: Delegate filesystems opened with fs.open_fs never close the base filesystem
issue_body:
When using `fs.open_fs` to open a FS URL with an associated directory, the returned filesystem is a `SubFS`. Those are made not to close the delegate filesystem, which is perfectly fine because else the following code:
```python
with my_fs.opendir("/some/path") as dirfs:
    # ... do stuff┬Ā...
```
would cause `my_fs` to be closed otherwise.

**BUT** the side effect is that, if you open a resource with a path, then the filesystem will not be closed when you think you are closing it.

```python
from fs import open_fs

ebi_ftp = open_fs("ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/phd")
ebi_ftp.close()       # the actual FTPFS is not closed since ebi_ftp is a SubFS

ebi_ftp.isclosed()  # True
ebi_ftp.delegate_fs().is_closed() # False
```

So, when you expect a context manager to close the filesystem, it actually doesn't:
```python
with open_fs("ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases") as ebi_db:
     # ... blah blah ... #

print("Done working on the remote FTP server !)
print("I'm happy the FTP connection was closed automatically thanks to Pyfilesystem2")
# It wasn't.
```

issue_title: IAR - Need to add a template for .ewd file
issue_body:
The debugger setting file is .ewd for the IAR IDE.
The .ewd file is not a template, but generated by a project generator regardless target and no way to configured.
The generated .ewd file is for cortex-M, and is not suitable for GR-PEACH (Cortex-A9) debug setting.
Therefore, users of GR-PEACH have to modify CMSIS-DAP section for debugger manually.
To configure .ewd file, please add a template for .ewd file.

We want to modify following:
- value of "state" in "CMSISDAPResetList"
- value of "state" in "CMSISDAPInterfaceRadio"

The former specifies the reset method. The value "10" is not valid to cortex-A9. Threfore IDE choose the default setting.
The latter specifies the interface is whether JTAG or SWD. I think that some platforms need to modify this setting.

issue_title: Where is the the Python (3) kernel?
issue_body:
**nteract** is working for me with the Python kernel but I have a question.

I think I have `kernel.json` in two locations:

1. `/usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/python3/kernel.json`
2. `/Users/lukejanicke/Library/Jupyter/kernels/python3/kernel.json`

Is that normal?

Here is my installation procedure.

1. I installed Python 3 with Homebrew.

```
brew install python3
```

2. [**nteract** website recommends](https://nteract.io/kernels/python) installing the Python kernel with the following commands.

```
python -m pip install ipykernel
python -m ipykernel install --user
```

The first command did not work for me.

```
~ $ python -m pip install ipykernel
/usr/bin/python: No module named pip
```

A few other attempts also failed.

```
~ $ python3 -m pip3 install ipykernel
/usr/local/opt/python3/bin/python3.6: No module named pip3
```

Until I found something that worked.

```
~ $ pip3 install ipykernel
Collecting ipykernel
. . .
Successfully installed appnope-0.1.0 decorator-4.1.2 ipykernel-4.7.0 ipython-6.2.1 ipython-genutils-0.2.0 jedi-0.11.1 jupyter-client-5.2.1 jupyter-core-4.4.0 parso-0.1.1 pexpect-4.3.1 pickleshare-0.7.4 prompt-toolkit-1.0.15 ptyprocess-0.5.2 pygments-2.2.0 python-dateutil-2.6.1 pyzmq-16.0.3 simplegeneric-0.8.1 six-1.11.0 tornado-4.5.3 traitlets-4.3.2 wcwidth-0.1.7
```

I later found that `python3 -m pip install ipykernel` also works.

At this point, `jupyter kernelspec list` gave the following output.

```
~ $ jupyter kernelspec list
Available kernels:
  python3    /usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/python3
```

I ran the next command from the [**nteract** website](https://nteract.io/kernels/python), changing `python` to `python3`.

```
~$ python3 -m ipykernel install --user
Installed kernelspec python3 in /Users/lukejanicke/Library/Jupyter/kernels/python3
```

At this point, I tried to run **nteract** and the Python kernel was not starting.

I ran `jupyter kernelspec list` again and noticed a different result!

```
$ jupyter kernelspec list
Available kernels:
  python3    /Users/lukejanicke/Library/Jupyter/kernels/python3
```

This confused me, because I remember from the last time I did this that I had to go into `kernel.json` and change the first `argv` from `python` to `python3`.

But now I had two `kernel.json` files.

1. `/usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/python3/kernel.json`
2. `/Users/lukejanicke/Library/Jupyter/kernels/python3/kernel.json`

`kernel.json` (2) already said `python3` and **nteract** was still not starting the Python kernel. So naturally, I looked at `kernel.json` (1). It still said `python`, so I changed that to `python3` as below.

```
{
 "argv": [
  "python3",
  "-m",
  "ipykernel_launcher",
  "-f",
  "{connection_file}"
 ],
 "display_name": "Python 3",
 "language": "python"
}
```

After this, the **nteract** started with the Python kernel and now seems to be working correctly.

Back to my question.

Why did `jupyter kernelspec list` give a different result between the following two commands?

```
pip3 install ipykernel
python3 -m ipykernel install --user
```

And why are there two `kernel.json` files?

issue_title: Cannot solve simple function

I suspect that the generated CFG might be an issue, since at least one basic block is reported by angr to be shorter than expected.

The script below first solves the problem by forcing the execution path to go through the basic block at 0x189 and then to 0x33e, the return instruction.

Then it tries to explore to 0x33e via simgr, which does not yield a path with rax != -1 (the error result).

''')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    with open('/tmp/sc', 'wb') as f:
        f.write(sc)

    proj = angr.Project('/tmp/sc', load_options = {
        'main_opts': {
            'backend': 'blob',
            'custom_arch': 'x86_64',
        },
    })

    print
    block = proj.factory.block(0x189)
    print 'I would expect that 0x{:x} == 0x324, the end of that basic block'.format(
            block.instruction_addrs[-1])

    print
    print 'Now lets find an input with return value 0'
    print

    print '{:x}'.format(st.se.eval(inp))

    sm = proj.factory.simgr(s)

issue_title: Multiple different Problems leading to Symexec .step() running infinite
issue_body:
Hello, 

another day - another headache problem from me. Analyzing a few thousand arm32 files, we stumbled upon an interesting bug which causes the symbolic execution to run infinite with a single `step()`. Sadly, there are multiple problems that lead to this problem.

First the code and the targets:

Starting-Point: `0x7754B9`
Find-Point: `0x007754D4` [JNI-Method]

Setup: 
```python
import logging
logging.basicConfig()
l = logging.getLogger()
l.setLevel(logging.INFO)

import angr

backend = "elf"
binarypath = "933b179091b73a5eb6cbe57639280f4880d3e37eb63f2c295e42f97c22ab70a6"
proj_options = {'auto_load_libs': False, 'main_opts': {'backend': backend}}
project = angr.Project(binarypath, load_options=proj_options, translation_cache = False)

angr_explorer_tech = angr.exploration_techniques.Explorer( \
            find=0x007754d5, \
            find_stash='found', \
            avoid_stash='avoid', \
            num_find=1)

state = project.factory.blank_state(addr=0x7754B9)
simgr = project.factory.simgr([state])
simgr.use_technique(angr_explorer_tech)
simgr.step()
``` 

![ida](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12801920/42690540-dc766186-86a4-11e8-95ae-842a7c4b8c7f.png)

```
.text:007754B8 ; djinni::Optional<std::experimental::optional, djinni::Bool>::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *, std::experimental::optional<bool> const&)
.text:007754B8                 WEAK _ZN6djinni8OptionalINSt12experimental8optionalENS_4BoolEE7fromCppEP7_JNIEnvRKNS2_IbEE
.text:007754B8 _ZN6djinni8OptionalINSt12experimental8optionalENS_4BoolEE7fromCppEP7_JNIEnvRKNS2_IbEE
.text:007754B8                                         ; CODE XREF: djinni_generated::NativeDbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,dropbox::product::dbapp::camera_upload::cu_engine::DbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata const&)+44Ōåōp
.text:007754B8                                         ; djinni_generated::NativeDbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,dropbox::product::dbapp::camera_upload::cu_engine::DbxExtendedFixedPhotoMetadata const&)+54Ōåōp ...
.text:007754B8                 PUSH            {R4-R6,LR}
.text:007754BA                 MOV             R4, R0
.text:007754BC                 LDRB            R3, [R2]
.text:007754BE                 MOV             R5, R1
.text:007754C0                 MOV             R6, R2
.text:007754C2                 CBZ             R3, loc_7754E4

.text:007754C4                 LDR             R3, =(loc_88D50C - 0x3754CC)
.text:007754C6                 MOV             R0, R1
.text:007754C8                 ADD             R3, PC  ; _ZN6djinni8JniClassINS_4BoolEE11s_singletonE_ptr
.text:007754CA                 LDR             R3, [R3] ; djinni::JniClass<djinni::Bool>::s_singleton
.text:007754CC                 LDR             R3, [R3] ; djinni::JniClass<djinni::Bool>::s_singleton
.text:007754CE                 LDMIA.W         R3, {R1,R2}
.text:007754D2                 LDRB            R3, [R6,#1]
.text:007754D4                 BL              _ZN7_JNIEnv22CallStaticObjectMethodEP7_jclassP10_jmethodIDz ; 
   ...
   ...
.text:007754E4 loc_7754E4                              ; CODE XREF: djinni::Optional<std::experimental::optional,djinni::Bool>::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,std::experimental::optional<bool> const&)+AŌåæj
.text:007754E4                 STR             R3, [R0]
.text:007754E6
.text:007754E6 loc_7754E6                              ; CODE XREF: djinni::Optional<std::experimental::optional,djinni::Bool>::fromCpp(_JNIEnv *,std::experimental::optional<bool> const&)+2AŌåæj
.text:007754E6                 MOV             R0, R4
.text:007754E8                 POP             {R4-R6,PC}

..
...
0x76C772 ; _JNIEnv::CallStaticObjectMethod(_jclass *, _jmethodID *, ...)
```

Problem 1: Exception in explorer, no successors:
```
Replacing explorer.py :: 127
state = self.project.factory.successors(state, num_inst=1).successors[0]

With:
succs = self.project.factory.successors(state, num_inst=1)
                    try:
                        if succs.successors:
                            state = succs.successors[0]
                    except SimIRSBNoDecodeError as ex:
                        if state.arch.name.startswith('MIPS'): 
                        ...
```

The solution may not be handy or sound, even faulty - but it brings the real problem to the top.


```python
Before Step: Active -> [0x7754B9]
After-Step: 
expected: active -> [0x7754E5, 0x7754C5] (At branching location, fallthrough branching)
faulty: active -> [0x7754E5, 0x76C773]
```
The step function at this point executes the entire basic block (which has incorrect boundaries, e.g. does not stop block at `CBZ`. While executing until the `BL` not stoping at `CBZ` it also ignores the `find addr`. Even more problematic, it also ignores setting the `LR` at `BL`. Hitting the return in the successive blocks it starts again at the beginning, thus leading to an un-interruptable infinite loop with a single `step()`.   

The same behaviour can be seen at `CBNZ` as well. 

Binary:
[933b179091b73a5eb6cbe57639280f4880d3e37eb63f2c295e42f97c22ab70a6.zip](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/2192463/933b179091b73a5eb6cbe57639280f4880d3e37eb63f2c295e42f97c22ab70a6.zip)

Any-Ideas?

Best reagrds,
Andreas

issue_title: SSH(x) to SSH(x) dumps x locally
issue_body:
In some testing today I found that SSH(JSON) to SSH(JSON) ends up dumping the JSON file in question in the local path. It turns out that odo uses the `append_sshX_to_X` function here under the `(JSON, SSH(JSON))` append signature, because the destination SSH(JSON) target is a subclass of JSON.

A quick fix to this could be just setting a rule that SSH(x) to SSH(x) raises `NotImplementedError`. The better solution long-term is probably something @mrocklin has suggested which is to refactor odo to separate the notions of data location and data format.

api = connect('127.0.0.1:14550')

api = connect('127.0.0.1:14550')

issue_title: get_next_passes returns max-elevation-time time not between rise & fall time
issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

```python

from pyorbital import tlefile
from pyorbital.orbital import Orbital
from datetime import datetime

TLE_FILE_NAME = 'iridium-tle.txt'

HORIZON = 40

# altitude (km) above seal-level and lat lon of the observer
ALTITUDE_ASL = 0.5
LON = 170.556
LAT = -43.368

tlefile.TLE_URLS = ('https://celestrak.com/NORAD/elements/iridium.txt', )
tlefile.fetch(TLE_FILE_NAME)

orb = Orbital('IRIDIUM 7 [+]', TLE_FILE_NAME)
d = datetime(2018, 3, 7, 3, 40, 15)
orb.get_next_passes(d, 1, LON, LAT, ALTITUDE_ASL, horizon=HORIZON)

```
#### Problem description

The max elevation time for a pass should lie between the rise and set times.

#### Expected Output

The documentation says get_next_passes returns " [(rise-time, fall-time, max-elevation-time), ŌĆ”]"

Max elevation time should lie between 
003:46:14 and 03:50:12

#### Actual Result, Traceback if applicable

issue_title: Unexpected gap between contiguous channels
issue_body:
When plotting a campaign using K2fov's `plotPointing`, a gap of ~10-15px is apparent in between channels that are expected to be contiguous.

For example, coordinate (ra, dec) = (185.87, -9.44924) falls inside the "gap" between channels 33 and 34 during Campaign 10.  There should be no gap between 33 and 34 however, they are the same ccd.

This behavior affects the functionality of `isOnSilicon`:
```
In [19]: from K2fov.fields import getKeplerFov
In [20]: c10fov = getKeplerFov(10)
In [21]: c10fov.isOnSilicon(185.87, -9.44924, 0)
Out[21]: False
```
(the expected result above is `True`)

The impact on target management is likely non-existent because of padding and inability to observe targets near edges.  However it may be worth understanding the behavior as it may expose a minor bug.

issue_body:
Should the GenericSplitter throw an exception if it fails to split?

And LHCb user posted that:

```
   splitter =    GenericSplitter (
     attribute = ,

and submitting it gets

jj.submit()

issue_title: Race condition in monitoring when copying jobs
issue_body:
In a clean Ganga session with a completely wiped `~/ganga_dir`, running the following:

```python
j = Job(); j.submit(); j.copy(); j.copy()
```

causes an exception in the monitoring loop about 80% of the time. The exception given is:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Job/Job.py", line 611, in updateStatus
    self.time.timenow(str(newstatus))
  File ".../ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Lib/Job/JobTime.py", line 127, in timenow
    be_statetime = j.backend.getStateTime(childstatus)
  File ".../ganga/python/Ganga/Lib/Localhost/Localhost.py", line 112, in getStateTime
    j = self.getJobObject()
  File ".../ganga/python/Ganga/GPIDev/Base/Objects.py", line 1025, in getJobObject
    raise AssertionError('no job associated with object ' + repr(self))
AssertionError: no job associated with object <Ganga.Lib.Localhost.Localhost.Localhost object at 0x314c650>
```

Pausing for a second between after the `submit()` operation makes the race condition disappear:

```python
from time import sleep
j = Job(); j.submit(); sleep(1); j.copy(); j.copy()
```

This came up when converting the JIRA1961 test to the new system.

issue_title: "Security Considerations" section in the docs.
issue_body:
We identified several issues which would be best practices when deploying a Kinto server:

* Use bucket id's that are unique to the group that has access to the collections in that bucket (i.e. don't use a "default" bucket that would make data from different users use identical URLs).
* Restrict the `"access-control-allow-origin"` header if the allowed origin(s) is/are known.
* Send a `"Cache-Control": "no-cache"` header on each response (this does not prevent caching, but it makes sure the browser cache does not leak info without triggering a revalidate and checking authorization with the server first.

There might be more things worth adding to this section.

issue_title: Create Parse migration tool for Kinto
issue_body: When a developer wants to migrate off [the Parse service](http://blog.parse.com/announcements/moving-on/), they need a tool to migrate their Parse data into Kinto, so they can more easily switch their app to Kinto.

issue_title: advice for ZR300 multicam setup / settings
issue_body:
I apologize before hand if this is not the right place to post this kind of question.

I'm currently attempting to do something similar to a real-time 3D scanner using a multicam setup.
I bought the ZR300 realsense due to this description in the datasheet: "In general, the ZR300 is not subject to infrared projector interference from multiple cameras in use simultaneously." ([source](https://click.intel.com/media/ZR300-Product-Datasheet-Public-002.pdf))

Now I have been running a small test setup using the multicam-cpp example bundled with librealsense with 2xZR300 and a 1xSR300 connected. I put a small video up of what I found [here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PVOFtoR_B58&feature=youtu.be). The two ZR300's are the left and middle windows, while the SR300 is the right-most windows. Looking at the depth image SR300 has a very nice clean depth image with almost no missing data (black pixels). However the two ZR300's have more noise and it becomes obvious (look at timestamp 0:35 to 1:40 in the video) that the areas which both IR projectors hit are blocking the depth measurements from for both ZR300 (creating black pixels). I find this a little odd thinking of the quote from the data sheet, but maybe I'm misreading "interference"?

I read in the data sheet that the active projector is only there to help in scenarios where texture is missing (e.g. measuring depth on a unicolored wall). Would it be possible to turn off the projectors and only rely on the stereo vision based depth? Do you have other suggestions for optimizations that I could do to improve on this multisensor setup?

 Thank you.

|---|---|
| Camera Model | ZR300 | 
| Firmware Version | No idea | 
| Operating System & Version | Windows 10 |
| Build System |  VS2017 Community |

issue_title: overflow for signed data where BitsStored is lower than BitsAllocated
issue_body:
For some Hitachi mri data it became apparent that there is an overflow issue.
Some investigation lead to a but in pydicom where it does not seem to handle cases where bitsstored and bitsallocated do not match. The overflow occurs because the for example a 12 bit signed integer is stored in a 16 bit space. As the sign bit is the most significant bit (12) we cannot read this data as if it where 16 bits.

We should:
- Provide a workaround
- Report a bug with pydicom

issue_title: ppo2: what's the point of creating 2 network
issue_body:
In ppo2.py, algorithm create 2 netowrk:

```
class Model(object):
    def __init__(self, *, policy, ob_space, ac_space, nbatch_act, nbatch_train,
                nsteps, ent_coef, vf_coef, max_grad_norm):
        sess = tf.get_default_session()
        global_step = tf.train.get_or_create_global_step()

        act_model = policy(sess, ob_space, ac_space, nbatch_act, 1, reuse=False)
        train_model = policy(sess, ob_space, ac_space, nbatch_train, nsteps, reuse=True)
```
act_model: for interaction with environments.
train_model: for training model.

but this 2 network share all variables, it makes them as 2 same network.
